articles.headline: How to Assess Safety Risk
articles.blurb: A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.
articles.takeaway: Part 5's structured risk assessment framework enables operators to quantify and prioritize safety hazards systematically rather than relying on intuition alone.

articles.headline: Tracking the next SDA challenge

articles.takeaway: Commercial space operators' proliferation of sensors and AI analytics is overwhelming government's ability to process actionable intelligence for space situational awareness.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Effective safety risk management requires organizations to document why certain residual risks remain acceptable after control implementation, not just identify hazards.

articles.headline: Preparing for the next NASA budget battle
articles.blurb: When Congress passed a fiscal year 2026 “minibus” appropriations bill in January, much of the space community breathed a sigh of relief. Congress had rejected the steep cuts proposed by the Trump administration, including a nearly 25% reduction in the agency’s overall budget and nearly 50% to...
articles.takeaway: NASA's reprieve from proposed cuts signals Congress's willingness to protect space exploration funding despite executive pressure, but sustained advocacy will be critical as budget battles continue beyond 2026.

articles.headline: This Airline Has The World's Best First Class In 2026

articles.headline: A lunar base or a lunar economy?
articles.blurb: We applaud the lunar base vision laid out by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman on March 24. Placing this stake in the lunar regolith is long overdue. What is missing from the mission architecture, however, is a vision for long-term economic development on the moon. It will be a profound missed...
articles.takeaway: Sustainable lunar infrastructure requires coupling NASA's base development with commercial economic models, not just government exploration capabilities.

articles.headline: Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

articles.takeaway: Wire strikes remain a leading fatal helicopter hazard despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, requiring systematic detection and avoidance training improvements.

articles.headline: What Do Airlines Really Do With Old Planes?
articles.blurb: The lifecycle of a commercial jet does not simply end when it is removed from a carrier's active schedule. The fate of these multi-million dollar machines is often seen as a mystery, yet understanding the retirement process is essential for grasping the true sustainability and economic scale of the...
articles.takeaway: Aircraft retirement represents a complex secondary market where planes generate significant residual value through conversion, leasing, or scrapping rather than immediate disposal.

articles.headline: The 5 Airlines With The World’s Most Modern Boeing 787 Cabins

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Integrated optimization across airline-airport-flight operations can simultaneously increase capacity and reduce emissions, addressing the dual pressure of rising demand and environmental regulations.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Industry navigates NASA’s start-and-stop approach to commercial space stations
articles.blurb: (Note: This article went to press for the April issue of SpaceNews Magazine before NASA announced potential changes to the CLD program.) NASA signaled last summer it planned to accelerate efforts to replace the aging International Space Station. Nearly nine months later, that push appears to have...
articles.takeaway: Inconsistent NASA funding and policy decisions for commercial space station development create investment uncertainty that could delay private sector readiness for ISS transition.

articles.headline: Artemis 2 science gets underway as Orion begins its return trip
